response.justification: James Smith is a seasoned full-stack and systems software engineer with over 15 years of experience across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency backend systems. However, the Principal Performance Architect role demands deep expertise in SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, and deep learning workload optimization — none of which appear anywhere in this resume. His background is entirely software-side, with no exposure to GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD pipelines, or silicon-level performance analysis. The closest relevant signal is low-latency DLang work at Dunnhumby and recent AI-agent tooling at re:cinq, but these are far removed from the hardware-oriented depth the role requires.
strengths: Demonstrated performance-conscious engineering in high-throughput, low-latency microservices (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Broad technical range across systems, embedded, and infrastructure layers, showing adaptability
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling at re:cinq, indicating growing familiarity with AI-adjacent work
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or chip-level performance analysis — the core of this role
No background in deep learning workloads, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator optimization (preferred and required qualifications are entirely absent)
Holds only a BS in Computer Science; the role requires BS + 8 years of *relevant* technical experience, and none of his experience is in hardware or AI performance domains

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware architecture experience
No deep learning or GPU workload expertise
